channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Helicopter crashes into radio tower in Houston, killing 4
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Four people aboard a helicopter were killed when it crashed into a radio tower in Houston and burst into flames, officials said. The helicopter went down just before 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Walmart and Aldi are once again offering meal deals for Thanksgiving
item.description: The two companies are advertising their dinners at "inflation-free" or "pre-inflation" prices, respectively.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KREX 5

item.title: Yankees and Dodgers meet in World Series for 12th time in matchup of Broadway and Hollywood
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Broadway vs. Hollywood. Subway vs. Freeway. Judge vs. Ohtani. New York neighbors who became cross-country rivals, the Yankees and Dodgers renew their starry struggle in the World Series for the first time in 43 years.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump bashes FEMA, NC governor as he tours Helene storm damage
item.description: Former President Trump on Monday attacked the federal response to Hurricane Helene by repeating debunked claims about funding and bashed North Carolina Gov. Roy Cooper (D) during a stop in storm-ravaged Asheville, N.C.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Should the minimum wage be lower for workers who get tipped? Two states are set to decide
item.description: Voters in Arizona and Massachusetts are set to decide whether employers should be able to continue to pay tipped workers such as servers and bartenders a lower minimum wage than non-tipped workers.
item.dc:creator: Kavish Harjai, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge will hear arguments to block Louisiana's Ten Commandments display requirement in schools
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge on Monday will hear arguments on whether he should temporarily block a new Louisiana law requiring that the Ten Commandments be displayed in every public school classroom by Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Child labor violations on the rise, problem could get worse: report
item.description: Between 2011 and 2020, the rate of injuries among workers under 18 nearly doubled, a recent report says.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, Fox 5

item.title: Oscar brings winds, heavy rains to eastern Cuba after striking the Bahamas
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Tropical Storm Oscar brought heavy rains and winds to Cuba, an island already beleaguered by a massive power outage, late Sunday after brushing the Bahamas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Battleground Florida: FEMA funds
item.description: Tampa Bay is working to recover from two recent back-to-back hurricanes that impacted the state weeks away from the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Full list: Meat recalled over listeria concerns shipped to nearly 200 schools
item.description: Nearly 200 schools across the country received shipments of ready-to-eat meat products that have been recalled over possible listeria contamination, according to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: SEC fines Tennessee $100,000 for rushing the field after win over Alabama
item.description: While Tennessee was fined $100,000 when fans rushed the field after beating Alabama in 2022, this penalty is the school's first violation since the policy was revised last year.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, NewsNation

item.title: Calmer weather helps contain Oakland, California, fire that forced evacuations
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Firefighting crews took advantage of calmer weather Sunday to increase containment of a small wildfire in Oakland, California, that burned two homes and forced more than 500 people to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 Navy aviators declared dead after fighter jet crash in Washington state
item.description: The aviators' names won’t be released until a day after their next of kin have been notified, the Navy said in a statement Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: One of the last Navajo Code Talkers from World War II dies at 107
item.description: John Kinsel Sr., one of the last remaining Navajo Code Talkers who transmitted messages during World War II based on the tribe's native language, has died. He was 107.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Many gift-buying parents pressured to overspend this holiday season: Survey
item.description: (NerdWallet) - Holiday shopping season is upon us, and the pressure is on, or at least it feels that way for some parents.
item.dc:creator: Erin El Issa, ABC 4

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Some electricity was restored in Cuba, the government said Saturday, after the island nation's worst blackout in at least two years left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Alec Baldwin returns to 'Saturday Night Live' to spoof Bret Baier interview with Kamala Harris
item.description: Alec Baldwin has returned to “Saturday Night Live.” But the actor who won an Emmy for playing former President Donald Trump didn't reprise that role — he returned Saturday night to play Fox News' Bret Baier in an opening skit that spoofed this week's interview with Kamala Harris.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A drone targets the Israeli prime minister's house during new barrages with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel's government said a drone targeted Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s house Saturday, with no casualties, as fighting with Lebanon-based Hezbollah and Gaza -based Hamas showed no pause after the killing of the Hamas mastermind of last year’s Oct. 7 attack.
item.dc:creator: ADAM SCHRECK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NYC officials envision turning Fifth Avenue into a grand boulevard
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Manhattan’s famed luxury store row Fifth Avenue is in line for a major makeover. New York City officials unveiled a plan this week to transform a central portion of the thoroughfare between Bryant Park and Central Park into a more pedestrian-centered boulevard.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mississippi couple celebrates 60 years of marriage at Chick-Fil-A
item.description: A Mississippi couple celebrated their 60th marriage anniversary at Chick-fil-A after eating lunch at the restaurant every day for the past two years.
item.dc:creator: Shay Simon, Fox 5

item.title: Stanford psychologist behind the controversial "Stanford Prison Experiment" dies at 91
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Philip G. Zimbardo, the psychologist behind the controversial “Stanford Prison Experiment” that was intended to examine the psychological experiences of imprisonment, has died. He was 91. Stanford University announced Friday that Zimbardo died Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Yankees get to Guardians closer Clase again, score 2 runs in 9th to win 8-6 and take 3-1 ALCS lead
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Cleveland All-Star closer Emmanuel Clase gave up two runs in the ninth inning and the New York Yankees moved closer to another World Series, beating the Guardians 8-6 on Friday night to take a 3-1 lead in the American League Championship Series.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump microphone cuts out for 20 minutes at Detroit rally
item.description: Trump was in the midst of speaking about his tariff policy at the event on Friday, about to call it “the most beautiful word in the dictionary,” before the microphone went down. Someone handed the former president another microphone to try, but it didn’t work either.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, ABC 4

item.title: Iowa school shooter ‘likely displayed warning signs’ before January attack, report finds
item.description: The shooter responsible for two deaths and multiple injuries at an Iowa high school earlier this year “likely displayed warning signs” according to a detailed report from state officials released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: California woman slashed dog's throat, left it alive in trash: Sacramento deputies
item.description: The woman "felt her life would be easier if she only had to care for one dog and cut one of the dog’s throats," say officials.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Catlin, WGN 9

item.title: Cuba’s grid goes offline with massive blackout after a major power plant fails
item.description: Cuba’s electrical grid went down Friday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, a day after a massive blackout swept across the Caribbean island and with no official estimate for when service will be restored.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Walter White of 'Breaking Bad' returns for New Mexico litter campaign
item.description: Two television ads featuring Bryan Cranston as Walter White, directed by "Breaking Bad" creator Vince Gilligan, were created to promote clean-up of New Mexico areas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Salinas, WGN 9

item.title: Big tobacco companies reach tentative multibillion-dollar settlement with Canada
item.description: Three large tobacco companies would pay nearly $24 billion to settle a long-running legal battle in Canada, according to a proposed deal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Video and an unused bullet prove man's guilt in Indiana girls' killings, prosecutor says
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. (AP) — A man charged with killing two teenage girls in a small Indiana community forced them off a hiking trail before cutting their throats, a prosecutor said Friday, telling jurors that the evidence includes an unused bullet and video recorded on the eldest girl’s phone.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump compares imprisoned Jan. 6 rioters to Japanese Americans during WWII
item.description: Nearly 900 people have pleaded guilty to a variety of federal charges in relation to the events of Jan. 6.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Trump tells child there will be no cows under Harris
item.description: Former President Donald Trump joined Fox and Friends on Friday for an in-person interview where he told a 6-year-old child that the U.S. “won’t have any cows” if Vice President Kamala Harris is elected.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Florida digs out of mountains of sand swept in by back-to-back hurricanes
item.description: The fine, white sand helps make Florida's beaches among the best in the world. But the powerful storms have turned the precious commodity into a costly nuisance, with sand creating literal barriers to recovery as homeowners and municipalities dig their way out.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne, WFLA, NBC
